medications.indication_rationale: Resume the patient's prescribed hydroxychloroquine at the usual dose, as missed doses likely precipitated the flare (do not exceed the prescribed dose).
Anti-inflammatory analgesia for the articular flare, with food, if no contraindication; caution/avoid if any renal concern.
medications.dose_route_frequency: e.g. naproxen 500 mg PO with food BD as needed (adult, if no contraindication)

medications.indication_rationale: Escalating immunosuppression is a rheumatology decision, not an acute telehealth initiation.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Resume hydroxychloroquine consistently, use warm packs on the sore joints, rest, hydrate, and ask your sister to check in overnight.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Addresses the likely trigger and provides a safety check given she lives alone.

patient_education_points.point: This is a joint-and-fatigue lupus flare, most likely because you missed some hydroxychloroquine doses. Your screening is reassuring — no chest pain on breathing, normal urine, and no single hot swollen joint — so your organs are not being damaged tonight.
Restart your hydroxychloroquine at your usual dose, use an anti-inflammatory with food and warm packs, rest and hydrate. Book urgent GP/rheumatology review in the next couple of days.
Go to ED if you get sharp chest pain on breathing or breathlessness, your urine turns cola/red-coloured, a single joint becomes bright red, hot and agony to move, or you develop a high fever.

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Flare activity and organ screen (bloods/urinalysis), medication adherence, and escalation of therapy if needed.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Serositis, nephritis (cola urine), or a septic joint — ED.

must_include_items: Screen serositis, nephritis and septic arthritis before reassuring
Restart hydroxychloroquine + NSAIDs + rest for an articular flare
Organ-crisis/septic-joint safety-net with a low threshold for review (lives alone)
errors_of_commission: ED over-triage of an organ-negative articular flare
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: T2 case: passing requires an organ/septic-joint screen + restart hydroxychloroquine/NSAIDs + safety-net with a low threshold for review. Over-triage or unscreened dismissal fail.
